terms.definition: Group of four muscles and tendons stabilising the glenohumeral joint and enabling shoulder movement.
terms.exampleSentence: Palpation reproduced pain consistent with a rotator cuff tear.
terms.contextNotes: Common source of shoulder pain in overhead athletes.

terms.definition: Intra-articular knee ligament resisting anterior tibial translation and rotational instability.
terms.exampleSentence: MRI confirmed a complete anterior cruciate ligament rupture.
terms.contextNotes: Often injured in non-contact pivoting sports.

terms.definition: Knee ligament preventing posterior translation of the tibia relative to the femur.
terms.exampleSentence: A positive posterior drawer test suggested posterior cruciate ligament injury.
terms.contextNotes: Typically injured by dashboard impact in road traffic accidents.

terms.definition: Ligament on the inner aspect of the knee resisting valgus stress.
terms.exampleSentence: Valgus laxity at thirty degrees indicated a grade two medial collateral ligament sprain.
terms.contextNotes: Often injured in contact sports from a lateral blow.

terms.definition: Ligament on the outer aspect of the knee resisting varus stress.
terms.exampleSentence: Varus stress testing reproduced pain over the lateral collateral ligament.
terms.contextNotes: Less commonly injured than the MCL.

terms.definition: C-shaped fibrocartilage in the knee that cushions and distributes load between femur and tibia.
terms.exampleSentence: Joint line tenderness raised suspicion of a medial meniscus tear.
terms.contextNotes: Plural is menisci; vascular zone determines healing potential.

terms.definition: Thick fibrous band on the sole of the foot supporting the medial longitudinal arch.
terms.exampleSentence: Tenderness was localised to the medial calcaneal insertion of the plantar fascia.
terms.contextNotes: Inflammation here causes characteristic first-step morning heel pain.

terms.definition: Thick fibrous band running along the lateral thigh from iliac crest to lateral tibia.
terms.exampleSentence: Lateral knee pain on running was attributed to iliotibial band friction syndrome.
terms.contextNotes: Common cause of lateral knee pain in long-distance runners.

terms.definition: Posterior thigh muscle group comprising biceps femoris, semitendinosus and semimembranosus.
terms.exampleSentence: Acute deceleration produced a grade two hamstrings strain.
terms.contextNotes: Knee flexors and hip extensors; commonly strained in sprinting.

terms.definition: Anterior thigh muscle group of four heads acting as the principal knee extensor.
terms.exampleSentence: Quadriceps wasting was evident two weeks post arthroscopy.
terms.contextNotes: Vastus medialis oblique is targeted in patellofemoral rehab.

terms.definition: Rotator cuff muscle initiating shoulder abduction in the first fifteen degrees.
terms.exampleSentence: An empty can test reproduced pain consistent with supraspinatus tendinopathy.
terms.contextNotes: Most frequently torn rotator cuff tendon.

terms.definition: Rotator cuff muscle producing external rotation of the shoulder.
terms.exampleSentence: External rotation strength was reduced, indicating infraspinatus involvement.
terms.contextNotes: Wasting visible posterior to scapular spine in chronic tears.

terms.definition: Small rotator cuff muscle assisting with external rotation of the shoulder.
terms.exampleSentence: Hornblower's sign suggested teres minor dysfunction.
terms.contextNotes: Innervated by axillary nerve; spared in suprascapular neuropathy.

terms.definition: Rotator cuff muscle on the anterior scapula producing internal rotation of the shoulder.
terms.exampleSentence: A positive lift-off test indicated subscapularis weakness.
terms.contextNotes: Only rotator cuff muscle on the anterior scapula.

terms.definition: Triangular shoulder muscle with anterior, middle and posterior fibres abducting the arm.
terms.exampleSentence: Mid-deltoid pain was reproduced on resisted abduction.
terms.contextNotes: Innervated by axillary nerve; vulnerable in shoulder dislocation.

terms.definition: Two-headed superficial calf muscle producing plantarflexion and assisting knee flexion.
terms.exampleSentence: An audible pop on push-off was consistent with a gastrocnemius tear.
terms.contextNotes: Tested in lengthened position with knee extended.

terms.definition: Deep calf muscle producing plantarflexion independent of knee position.
terms.exampleSentence: Pain on bent-knee calf raise localised the lesion to the soleus.
terms.contextNotes: Isolated by testing plantarflexion with the knee flexed.

terms.definition: Strongest tendon in the body, joining gastrocnemius and soleus to the calcaneus.
terms.exampleSentence: A positive Thompson test confirmed Achilles tendon rupture.
terms.contextNotes: Watershed area 2-6 cm above insertion is most prone to rupture.

terms.definition: Lower five vertebrae of the vertebral column between the thorax and sacrum.
terms.exampleSentence: Plain radiographs of the lumbar spine showed degenerative changes at L4 to L5.
terms.contextNotes: Common site of disc prolapse and facet joint arthropathy.

terms.definition: Upper seven vertebrae of the vertebral column forming the bony neck.
terms.exampleSentence: Cervical spine range of motion was globally restricted.
terms.contextNotes: C1 to C7; C1-C2 provides most rotation.

terms.definition: Twelve middle vertebrae of the vertebral column articulating with the ribs.
terms.exampleSentence: Thoracic spine extension mobility improved with thoracic mobilisations.
terms.contextNotes: Reduced extension contributes to shoulder impingement.

terms.definition: Fibrocartilaginous cushion between adjacent vertebral bodies allowing movement and shock absorption.
terms.exampleSentence: MRI demonstrated a posterolateral L5/S1 intervertebral disc herniation.
terms.contextNotes: Comprises nucleus pulposus and annulus fibrosus.

terms.definition: Synovial joint between superior and inferior articular processes of adjacent vertebrae.
terms.exampleSentence: Lumbar extension reproduced unilateral facet joint pain.
terms.contextNotes: Provoked by extension and ipsilateral side bending.

terms.definition: Synovial-fibrous joint between the sacrum and ilium transmitting load between trunk and legs.
terms.exampleSentence: Three positive provocation tests indicated sacroiliac joint involvement.
terms.contextNotes: Common source of pelvic and lower buttock pain in pregnancy.

terms.definition: Ball-and-socket synovial joint between the humeral head and scapular glenoid.
terms.exampleSentence: Anterior glenohumeral joint instability was demonstrated on apprehension testing.
terms.contextNotes: Most mobile and most commonly dislocated joint in the body.

terms.definition: Sesamoid bone within the quadriceps tendon, articulating with the femoral trochlea.
terms.exampleSentence: Lateral patella tracking was observed during a single-leg squat.
terms.contextNotes: Largest sesamoid bone in the body.

terms.definition: Fibrocartilaginous rim deepening the glenoid or acetabulum to enhance joint stability.
terms.exampleSentence: MR arthrogram confirmed an anterior glenoid labrum tear.
terms.contextNotes: SLAP lesions involve the superior labrum from anterior to posterior.

terms.definition: Large superficial back muscle elevating, retracting and rotating the scapula.
terms.exampleSentence: Upper trapezius hypertonicity was noted bilaterally on palpation.
terms.contextNotes: Innervated by spinal accessory nerve (CN XI).

terms.definition: Deep hip flexor running from the lumbar spine to the lesser trochanter of the femur.
terms.exampleSentence: A positive Thomas test indicated psoas major tightness.
terms.contextNotes: Combines with iliacus to form iliopsoas, the principal hip flexor.

terms.definition: Lateral hip muscle abducting the femur and stabilising the pelvis in single-leg stance.
terms.exampleSentence: Trendelenburg sign was positive, suggesting gluteus medius weakness.
terms.contextNotes: Weakness contributes to dynamic knee valgus and lateral hip pain.
